figures.caption: Figure 2: Overview of TOPOTUNER. TOPOTUNER uses a prior fine-tuning task to compute the topological drift of attention projection matrices. The computed topological drift is used to (A) build reusable freezing profiles for selective fine-tuning on new datasets and tasks, and to (B) track structural change across epochs for early stopping. Freezing profiles are provided in Appendix B.

Table 2: Transferred knowledge. Fine-tuning results across SA:SST-2, SA:IMDB, and QA:MMLU. TOPOTUNER selective tuning freezes projections learned from QA:GSM8K. *: LoRA trains only 0.17–0.19% extra parameters, but after merging can affect up to 100% of the targeted projection matrices.
Figure 7: Reusable freezing profile for LLaMA-3.1-8B learned from QA:GSM8K.

Figure 8: Reusable Freezing Profiles. We define a reusable freezing profile learned from a priortask fine-tuning run. Each model card summarizes the model, prior task, candidate matrix set, freezing strategy, selected frozen IDs, and outcome on target datasets. We report three profiles used in our experiments: TDA-High3-LLaMA, TDA-High3-Qwen, and TDA-High3-Mistral.
Table 3: Training hyperparameters used in our fine-tuning experiments. Freeze denotes the selective tuning setting used by the Eltwise- and TDA-based methods.
Table 5: Normalized average projection ranks (rank divided by total count). Lower values indicate larger drift. LLaMA-3.1-8B/ Mistral-7B-v0.3 are normalized by 128, Qwen3-8B-Base by 144.

Table 6: Top-N projection counts under mean element-wise relative drift. Each cell reports K Q (K+Q) | V O (V+O) among the top-N most changed projections. LLaMA-3.1-8B/Mistral-7B-v0.3 runs contain 128 projection matrices from 32 layers, while Qwen3-8B-Base runs contain 144 projection matrices from 36 layers.
Table 7: Model weight-change statistics relative to the pretrained checkpoint. TDA-High3 uses 86.25% less parameter updates than LoRA across the 12 LLM-dataset settings.
Figure 9: Epoch-6 Eltwise distances for the V and O projection matrices under H0.
Figure 10: Epoch-6 Wasserstein distances for the V and O projection matrices under H0.
Figure 11: Topological drift of the V and O projection on QA:GSM8K under H0. The top row shows epoch-to-epoch drift bars, and the bottom row shows Wasserstein distance curves across layers.
Figure 12: Topological drift of the V and O projection on QA:MMLU under H0. The top row shows epoch-to-epoch drift bars, and the bottom row shows Wasserstein distance curves across layers.
Figure 13: Topological drift of the V projection on SA:IMDB under H0. The top row shows epochto-epoch drift bars, and the bottom row shows Wasserstein distance curves across layers.
Figure 14: Topological drift of the V and O projection on SA:SST-2 under H0. The top row shows epoch-to-epoch drift bars, and the bottom row shows Wasserstein distance curves across layers.
Table 9: LLaMA-3.1-8B on QA:GSM8K with separate V and O freezing. Among selective freezing settings, the best configuration is tied at TDA-High6 (%61.17) and Eltwise-Low9 (%61.17).

Table 10: Qwen3-8B-Base on QA:GSM8K with separate V and O freezing. Among selective freezing settings, the best configuration is TDA-High3 (%87.62), followed by Eltwise-High9 (%87.43) and Eltwise-Low12 (%87.40).
Table 11: Mistral-7B-v0.3 on QA:GSM8K with separate V and O freezing. Among selective freezing settings, the best configuration is TDA-Low6 (54.31%), followed by Eltwise-High6 (53.96%).
Figure 15: Accuracies on each epoch for all the models across datasets
Figure 16: Topology-based stopping epochs under full fine-tuning. The plots show selected stopping epochs across η thresholds for SA:IMDB and QA:MMLU across the three model families.

Table 15: Catastrophic forgetting evaluation on QA:GSM8K fine-tuned LLaMA-3.1-8B. Metrics are avg. ± std over three fixed, non-overlapping evaluation subsets. Base and full finetuned models are shown as reference rows. Bold indicates the better result between LoRA and TOPOTUNER for each metric.

Table 16: Catastrophic forgetting evaluation on QA:GSM8K fine-tuned Qwen3-8B-Base. Metrics are avg. ± std over three fixed, non-overlapping evaluation subsets. Base and full finetuned models are shown as reference rows. Bold indicates the better result between LoRA and TOPOTUNER for each metric.

Table 17: Catastrophic forgetting evaluation on QA:GSM8K fine-tuned Mistral-7B-v0.3. Metrics are avg. ± std over three fixed, non-overlapping evaluation subsets. Base and full finetuned models are shown as reference rows. Bold indicates the better result between LoRA and TOPOTUNER for each metric.
